60 km wind, thunderstorm, heavy rain and…: Delhi, UP, Bihar, Jharkhand, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and other 14 states to…; check IMD full forecast here
The weather across the country has become highly unpredictable and extreme. While many parts of North and Central India are battling intense heatwave conditions, several other regions are witnessing thunderstorms and heavy rainfall due to strengthening monsoon activity and cyclonic circulation systems. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued alerts for more than 14 states, warning of heavy rain, lightning, and powerful thundersqualls with wind speeds reaching up to 60 kilometers per hour until May 25.
The weather across the country has become highly unpredictable and extreme. While many parts of North and Central India are battling intense heatwave conditions, several other regions are witnessing thunderstorms and heavy rainfall due to strengthening monsoon activity and cyclonic circulation systems. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued alerts for more than 14 states, warning of heavy rain, lightning, and powerful thundersqualls with wind speeds reaching up to 60 kilometers per hour until May 25.
Thunderstorm and Strong Wind Alert in Several States
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned of dangerous thunderstorms and strong winds in many states. Bihar may face severe storms on May 19, 20, and 23, while Jharkhand is likely to witness strong storms on May 20 and 23. Heavy squalls are also expected in West Bengal and Odisha between May 19 and 21.
Heavy Rain Expected in South and Northeast India
Several southern and northeastern states are likely to receive heavy to very heavy rainfall over the next few days. Kerala, Tamil Nadu, coastal Karnataka, Lakshadweep, and nearby regions may witness heavy rain between May 19 and 21. In Northeast India, states like Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura are expected to receive intense rainfall between May 22 and 25.
Very Heavy Rainfall Alert for Hilly Areas
Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim are expected to receive heavy rainfall from May 21 to 25, with extremely heavy rain likely on May 22 and 23. Arunachal Pradesh and parts of Assam and Meghalaya may also witness extremely heavy rainfall. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are expected to receive continuous rain throughout the week.
Severe Heatwave Continues in North and Central India
Even as many states face rain and storms, North and Central India will continue to experience extreme heatwave conditions. Western Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh may face severe heat till May 25, while eastern Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha are expected to remain under heatwave conditions till May 21. Delhi-NCR, Punjab, and Haryana may also continue to witness intense heat, with some areas likely to face severe heatwave conditions on May 19 and 20.
IMD Warning and Safety Advice
The weather department has warned that heavy rain may cause waterlogging, traffic jams, low visibility, and damage to crops and weak structures. Hilly areas, especially in Northeast India and Sikkim, may face landslides and mudslides. The IMD has advised people to check traffic updates before travelling, avoid waterlogged roads, and stay away from weak or damaged buildings during bad weather.
